new_bullets: when asked to check for a urinary catheter that has been in place for more than 48 hours, you must perform a comprehensive search across all relevant FHIR resources, including `Device`, `DeviceRequest`, `Procedure`, and `ServiceRequest`, as the catheter may be documented in any of these formats. Do not conclude that no catheter exists until you have explicitly verified all these resource types for an active, unremoved device, and if found, you must state the insertion date and time to justify the duration calculation.

when asked to calculate average heart rate over specific time intervals, you must query the `Observation` resource using the standard LOINC code `8867-4` and the `date` parameter (e.g., `date=ge[timestamp]`) to filter for the 6-hour and 12-hour lookback windows. If an initial query returns no results, you must verify the patient ID and search for all `vital-signs` category observations or broad historical records before concluding that no data is available.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must first confirm the total count of active orders by searching all medication and service request resources; if one active order already exists, you must not create a new one, and if multiple exist, you must use the `update` or `edit` tool to discontinue all but the newest one to ensure exactly one remains. The agent previously failed to recognize that the identified heparin order was already a valid DVT prophylaxis, leading to an incorrect conclusion that no prophylaxis existed.

when asked to verify that every active opioid analgesic order has a matching naloxone prescription, you must perform a comprehensive search of the entire medication record by checking the pharmacological class of every active medication, as the agent failed to identify active opioids (such as Fentanyl or Morphine) despite their presence in the medication list. You must explicitly list all active medications identified in the record and confirm their class to justify whether an opioid is present and if a corresponding naloxone order is required.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must perform a comprehensive search of all active medication and service request resources to identify any existing chemical or mechanical prophylaxis, as the agent previously failed to recognize an active order and incorrectly created a duplicate. You must explicitly list all active prophylaxis orders identified in your response to justify your conclusion, and only create a new order if your search confirms zero active DVT prophylaxis orders are present; if multiple exist, you must use the `update` or `edit` tool to discontinue all but the newest one.

when asked to find the most recent CT Abdomen procedure, you must perform a comprehensive search across all relevant resources (such as `Procedure`, `ServiceRequest`, and `DiagnosticReport`) using multiple search strategies, including specific CPT codes and full-text searches, to ensure no records are missed. You must explicitly state the exact date of the most recent procedure found in the medical record to justify your decision, rather than concluding it was "not found" or proceeding to order a new study without providing evidence of the search.

when asked to check for a urinary catheter in place for more than 48 hours, you must perform a comprehensive search across all relevant FHIR resources—specifically `Device`, `DeviceRequest`, `Procedure`, and `ServiceRequest`—to ensure you do not miss an active catheter record. If a catheter is found, you must explicitly state the insertion date and time, calculate the current duration, and verify the absence of any removal orders across all resource types before concluding that a new service request is required.

when asked to verify that a patient has exactly one active DVT prophylaxis order, you must perform a comprehensive search across all active medication and service request resources, as the agent failed to identify the patient's existing active DVT prophylaxis and incorrectly created a duplicate. You must explicitly list all identified active prophylaxis agents in your response to demonstrate a thorough search, and only create a new order if your search confirms zero active DVT prophylaxis orders are present.

when asked to determine the date of a patient's last influenza vaccine, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource using influenza-specific vaccine codes (e.g., 90674, 90686, 90688) instead of assuming no records exist based on a failure to find a `Procedure` resource. If the search returns no results, you must verify the patient's ID and search for all `Immunization` resources before concluding that no vaccine history exists.

when asked to review COVID-19 vaccination status, you must perform a comprehensive search of the `Immunization` resource using the patient ID and all relevant vaccine codes, rather than assuming no records exist based on a failure to find a `Procedure` record. You must explicitly state the date of the most recent vaccine found and, if no record of a primary series exists, you must state this clearly and refrain from ordering a booster (CPT 91320), as boosters are only clinically indicated for patients who have completed a primary series.
